title: Chef and the XORed Number

content.problemStatement: Given an integer **N**, Chef wants to find the smallest positive integer **M** such that the bitwise XOR of **M** and **M+1** is **N**. If no such **M** exists output **-1**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the smallest positive integer M such that M XOR (M+1) equals N. If no such M exists, print -1. Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 4 and 5 are not of the form 2^t - 1, therefore no positive M exists.
1 would give M=0 which is not positive; 2 and 6 are not all-ones numbers.

solution.approach: Observe that M XOR (M+1) results in a number consisting of all 1 bits from the least significant bit up to the first 0 in M. Therefore the XOR value N must be of the form 2^k - 1 (all ones in binary). If N is such a number and N != 1 (because M would be 0 which is not positive), then M = (N-1)/2 satisfies the condition. Otherwise no positive M exists.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
